SOURCES SOUGHT NOTICE

Agency: U.S. Army Corps of Engineers (USACE), Savannah District
Synopsis Number: W912HN-25-SS-0043
Title: Facility Operations and Maintenance Support Services – Fort Stewart and Hunter Army Airfield, Georgia
NAICS Code: 561210 – Facilities Support Services
Size Standard: $47.0M
Response Deadline: February 14, 2025, 4:00 PM ET

The U.S. Army Corps of Engineers, Savannah District, is conducting market research to determine the availability of qualified SBA-certified HUBZone small business concerns capable of providing comprehensive facility operations and maintenance services at Fort Stewart and Hunter Army Airfield, Georgia. Services include HVAC maintenance, plumbing, electrical systems repair, grounds maintenance, pest control, and refuse collection across approximately 1,200 facilities.

Based on market research results, the Government anticipates issuing this requirement as a HUBZone set-aside in accordance with FAR 19.1305 if two or more responsible HUBZone small business concerns are identified that can perform the work at fair market prices. The HUBZone price evaluation preference in accordance with FAR 19.1307 may also apply.

Interested firms must submit capability statements addressing the following:

(a) Company name, CAGE code, DUNS/UEI number, and current SBA HUBZone certification status (include certification date and SBA case number);
(b) Relevant past performance on contracts of similar size, scope, and complexity performed within the last five (5) years, including contract numbers, dollar values, and points of contact;
(c) Description of technical capability and available resources;
(d) Confirmation of ability to meet bonding requirements up to $5,000,000.

This notice is for market research and planning purposes only and does not constitute a solicitation or commitment by the Government to issue a solicitation or award a contract. No reimbursement will be made for any costs associated with providing information in response to this notice.

AMENDMENT OF SOLICITATION/MODIFICATION OF CONTRACT
U.S. Department of Agriculture – Agricultural Research Service
Solicitation No.: AG-6395-S-25-0021
Amendment No.: 0003

Purpose of Amendment: This amendment is issued to clarify the set-aside determination and to incorporate additional questions and answers from the pre-proposal conference held on March 12, 2025.

1. SET-ASIDE CLARIFICATION:

The Contracting Officer confirms that this acquisition for Laboratory Information Management System (LIMS) Software Development and Support Services is set aside for Women-Owned Small Business (WOSB) concerns under the WOSB Program, as authorized by FAR 19.1505. This is NOT an Economically Disadvantaged Women-Owned Small Business (EDWOSB) set-aside. The solicitation is restricted to WOSB-certified firms only.

NAICS: 541511 – Custom Computer Programming Services
Size Standard: $34.0 million

Offerors must hold a current, valid WOSB certification issued by the SBA at the time of offer and at the time of award. The firm must be unconditionally owned and controlled by one or more women who are U.S. citizens, and must be small under the assigned NAICS code. The clause at FAR 52.219-29, Notice of Set-Aside for, or Sole-Source Award to, Women-Owned Small Business Concerns Eligible Under the Women-Owned Small Business Program, is applicable and is hereby incorporated by reference.

2. QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS:

Q7: Can a joint venture between a WOSB and a large business submit a proposal?
A7: No. Only firms that are themselves certified WOSB concerns under the SBA's WOSB Program are eligible. Joint ventures must independently qualify as WOSB concerns and meet the requirements of 13 CFR 127.506.

Q8: Is past performance under prior EDWOSB contracts acceptable as relevant experience?
A8: Yes, past performance references from EDWOSB or WOSB contracts are acceptable as relevant experience for evaluation under Factor 2.

All other terms and conditions remain unchanged.

Statement of Work
Cybersecure HM&E Control Systems & Networks Department
Scrum Master and Product Owner Training

1.0 INTRODUCTION
1.1 The Naval Surface Warfare Center Philadelphia Division (NSWCPD) is a Department of War entity responsible for research and development, test and evaluation, engineering and fleet support organization for the Navy's ships, submarines, military watercraft and unmanned vehicles. This requirement is for NSWCPD Dept 50, which is responsible for Cybersecure Hull, Machinery & Electronic (HM&E) Control Systems & Networks Department.

1.2 BACKGROUND
The Cybersecure Machinery Controls Systems & Networks Department is the principal provider of life cycle management (LCM) and full spectrum engineering support for cybersecure surface ship machinery information, sensors and control systems; mission-critical networks; steering and integrated bridge/navigation systems. Scrum has been chosen as a framework across multiple programs to manage requirements and tasks associated with those requirements.

1.3 SCOPE OF WORK
The Vendor shall provide a three-day course on site at NSWCPD that will provide training in the roles of Scrum Team, Registered Scrum Master and Registered Product Owner. At the completion of the course and satisfactory participation by the individual attendees, the vendor will provide each attendee with both a Registered Scrum Master and Registered Product Owner certification exam password.

2.0 PERIOD OF PERFORMANCE
The Vendor shall provide access to the training services no later than 30 days upon Award.

REQUEST FOR QUOTATION (RFQ)
RFQ Number: DOI-BLM-NV-2025-0091
Issuing Office: Bureau of Land Management, Nevada State Office, Branch of Acquisition Management
Date Issued: January 22, 2025
Response Due: February 12, 2025, 4:00 PM Pacific Time

1. REQUIREMENT SUMMARY
The Bureau of Land Management (BLM), Nevada State Office, requires a qualified contractor to perform archaeological pedestrian survey and cultural resource inventory for approximately 2,500 acres of public land in Nye County, Nevada. The survey area is associated with a proposed solar energy right-of-way application (Serial Number NVN-099817) and must be completed prior to the National Environmental Policy Act (NEPA) environmental assessment.

2. APPLICABLE STANDARDS
All fieldwork shall be conducted in compliance with Section 106 of the National Historic Preservation Act (54 U.S.C. § 306108), 36 CFR Part 800, and the BLM 8100 Manual Series. The Principal Investigator must meet the Secretary of the Interior's Professional Qualification Standards (48 FR 44738-44739) in the discipline of archaeology.

3. CONTRACT TYPE AND VALUE
This procurement will result in a single firm-fixed-price purchase order. The total estimated contract value is $89,500.00 (Eighty-Nine Thousand Five Hundred Dollars). Quotations exceeding this amount may be considered non-responsive. This procurement is conducted under FAR Part 13 Simplified Acquisition Procedures and is set aside 100% for small businesses under NAICS 541720 (Research and Development in the Social Sciences and Humanities), size standard $19.5 million.

4. PERIOD OF PERFORMANCE
The period of performance shall not exceed 180 calendar days from the date of award.

5. DELIVERABLES
(a) Survey Research Design – 15 days ARO
(b) Weekly progress reports during active field survey
(c) Draft Cultural Resource Inventory Report (per BLM Manual 8110 format) – 120 days ARO
(d) Final Cultural Resource Inventory Report – 160 days ARO
(e) Site forms (IMACS format) for all newly recorded and re-recorded sites
(f) GIS geodatabase of all survey data (ESRI file geodatabase, NAD83 UTM Zone 11N)

6. QUOTATION SUBMISSION
Quotations shall be submitted electronically to blm_nv_acquisitions@blm.gov and must include: (i) completed price schedule, (ii) technical approach narrative (5-page limit), (iii) key personnel resumes, and (iv) three references for similar archaeological inventory projects on federal lands completed within the past five years.

The Team Integrator component of the CIRCLE program represents and highlights the overarching focus on integrated delivery of both research & development as well as commercialization/transition inherent to the CIRCLE program; this component is expected to play a management role and thus must be led by the performing teams' Project Leads (PLs). Team Integrators will identify TA-specific technologies and multi-TA integrations that may transition to the commercial marketplace and support the transition of integrated healthcare innovations.
• Program-wide TA integration and interaction with AP teams: Team Integrators will collaborate with the AP teams to reach consensus on the choice of data to be collected by the AP1 team. Similarly, Team Integrators will work to assure that the TA groups take advantage of common data products provided by the AP1 performer. Team Integrators will collaborate with the AP2 performer to incorporate TA2-generated models into standardized CIRCLE digital twin platform(s) for validation and future FDA evaluation.

SCOPE
A. The work of this section includes furnishing all water meters with MIUs and encoder register and a complete radio frequency based Automated Meter Reading (AMR) System or radio frequency based Advanced Metering Infrastructure (AMI) System. The system shall include furnish of water meters and registers, radio frequency components, data collection equipment and data collection software and training. The proposed system shall be implemented on new and existing residential, commercial, industrial and governmental customers.

B. The meter reading system shall consist of the necessary hardware and software to allow automatic meter readings and other data to be sent from the meter to a computer, handheld unit or internet, capable of downloading into the Owner's billing system. All meters, encoder registers and communications equipment will be furnished as part of this contract.

B. Experience managing similar projects of this magnitude and provide a minimum of three references.

C. Approved system suppliers are Mueller, Badger, Neptune, Sensus, Zenner or equal. One manufacturer shall supply both metering equipment (water meters) and radios (encoder registers with MIUs).

2.0 SCOPE OF WORK

The scope of this effort is to acquire services or capabilities for AFE capabilities focused on features identified by FG as important but challenging. This includes the provisioning, configuration, deployment, and sustainment of a commercial/out-of-the-box AFE capability to automate the identification and extraction of geospatial features from imagery and other source materials. AFE capabilities will be a critical enabler for modernizing Foundation GEOINT processes and will be integrated with key NGA systems. The work will support NGA's maritime, aeronautical, topographic, geographic, and geomatics mission domains and will be integrated into NGA's FG production environments on all required security domains.

This RFI seeks industry input on available out-of-the-box AFE capabilities and how they can be provided as a service to support NGA operations to automatically detect and extract geospatial features from various data input sources, including imagery and raster maps, to meet the objectives described within the AFE Statement of Objectives (SOO). NGA envisions this initiative to leverage the use of artificial intelligence and machine learning (AI/ML) to transform geospatial intelligence production, significantly reducing manual processing time, increasing data accuracy, and enabling the rapid delivery of intelligence to decision-makers.

III. SCOPE OF SERVICES AND FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS

Offerors must provide a detailed response to each requirement below, indicating if the feature/function is "Native/Included," "Third-Party Integration," or "Not Available" (Attachment A).

A. Core Functional Modules
1. Teacher & Student Portals: The system must provide a robust gradebook and portal interface for teachers and students (comparable in functionality and user experience to "TeacherVUE/StudentVUE/ParentVUE" or functional equivalent).
2. Mobile Accessibility: Dedicated iOS and Android mobile applications for Parents, Students, Teachers, and Administrators with push notification capabilities.
3. Real-Time Grading: Instant synchronization between teacher gradebooks and student/parent portals.
4. Attendance & Discipline: Comprehensive attendance tracking, including Kiosk late check-in functionality for front offices.
5. Mass Scheduling: Automated and manual master scheduling tools capable of handling complex district logic.
6. Online Registration: A paperless, secure, and mobile-friendly online registration and enrollment system for new and returning students.
7. Training: Offeror must provide a "Distance/Training portal" providing on-demand video tutorials, manuals, and certification paths for staff.

B. Operations & Safety Integration
1. Hall Pass Integration: Digital hall pass management or seamless integration with leading electronic hall pass systems.
2. Visitor Management: Integrated Visitor Check-in module or verified compatibility with standard visitor management hardware/software.
3. Incident Response: Vendor must guarantee a 24-hour response time to critical incidents (system outages, security breaches, or data corruption).
4. Badges: Use of student badges with attendance, events, hall pass, and other applicable systems.

The Aerospace Vehicles Division has been conducting Research and Development (R&D) programs through the Structures Engineering Research Program (SERP). SERP is an ID/IQ contract. 6.2.1 Subtask 1 – Product Backlog
The contractor shall develop and deliver an agile Product Backlog that is organized in a hierarchy where Epics are decomposed into Features and Features are decomposed into User Stories with bi-directional traceability between each level.

The contractor shall collaborate with key stakeholders, including the Government Product Owner to elicit, document, refine, and prioritize backlog items. Backlog items shall contain, at a minimum, the requisite information:
• Title
• Description
• Level of Effort (e.g., story points, hours, etc.)
• Definition of Done / Acceptance Criteria
• Dependencies
• Other attributes that characterize the story

Stories shall fall into one of four states:
• Proposed: The story is complete (contains the required attributes) but has not been approved by the Government Product Owner.
• Approved: The story has been approved, and work can begin.
• In-Progress: The story is approved, assigned to a team, and is currently under development.
• Done: The story has been completed (meeting the definition of done and all acceptance criteria) and is accepted by the Government Product Owner.

The contractor shall employ agile backlog grooming practices, including backlog refinement sessions, to continuously review, update, and reprioritize items based on stakeholder feedback, technical feasibility, and business value. The backlog shall be maintained in an agile project management tool designated by the Government.

B. Method of Award
This RFP is part of a competitive procurement process designed to serve the best interests of UPSTATE and the People of the State of New York. It is also designed to provide all bidders with a fair and even opportunity to have their services considered. UPSTATE will conduct a comprehensive review of each responsive bid submitted in accordance with the terms of this RFP. Proposals will be evaluated on the basis of "best value" by an evaluation committee comprised of UPSTATE representatives, and shall be conducted in the following steps:

3. Technical Evaluation (65 Points)- before presentation points
Each bid remaining after review of Bidder Qualifications will advance for review and independent evaluation and scoring by the technical review evaluation committee.

4. Financial Evaluation (25 Points)
The Financial Proposals of each bid remaining after the Technical Evaluation will be independently evaluated and scored by the designated contract officer.
Cost points will be awarded as follows: Lowest Total Contract Cost will receive twenty-five (25) points. All other bids will be awarded points based on a ratio of their cost to the lowest Total Contract Cost.

5. Presentation, Demonstration, Interview (10 Points)

6. Selection
Proposals will be evaluated on the basis of "best value" by an evaluation committee comprised of UPSTATE representatives. The Bidder earning the highest final composite score (technical, financial, and presentation/demonstration/interview vendor) will be selected to contract with UPSTATE.

SECTION M - EVALUATION FACTORS FOR AWARD

M.1. EVALUATION FACTORS FOR AWARD

a. The House intends to make one or more awards to Offerors whose proposals meet the requirements as stated in this solicitation. This will be a multi-phase evaluation. For the first phase proposals will be evaluated based on the following evaluation factors:

i. technical approach
ii. management approach
iii. corporate capabilities
iv. past performance

3.6 Claims Management
The system shall support registrant/Inductee data, end-to-end claims intake, processing, adjudication, reporting, and customer support.
Required Capabilities:
• Provide a secure public-facing portal allowing claimants to:
  o File claims
  o Submit supporting evidence and attachments
  o View agency-generated documents related to their claim
• Maintain a complete, immutable record of claim edits, actions, and decisions.
• Route claim packages requiring volunteer review based on claimant geographic location.
• Allow volunteers to securely access assigned claim packages using government-approved or authorized personal devices.
• Enforce strict protection of Personally Identifiable Information (PII) and Protected Health Information (PHI) at all times.
• Provide standard and ad hoc reporting on claims volume, processing times, outcomes, and adjudication trends.
• Provide trend analysis capabilities to identify patterns in claim submissions, processing timelines, outcomes, and recurring issues, including configurable analytics and visualizations to support operational and policy decision-making.

4) All ancillary equipment, hardware, software, and appurtenances needed for proper installation and operation of the SCADA equipment.
5) Provide spare parts and maintenance tools as described below.

7) All start-up labor and services.
